title: Skill4Econ Research Brief Output
description: Constrains research-intake briefs for applied economists. It supports scholarly_depth but does not imply causal proof, structural recovery, legal authority, audit readiness, or backend certification. Use claim_gate.json for claims; diagnostic_success is not paper-ready evidence; parser-only, interface-only, or missing_dependency is not a backend pass; official/latest sources are needed for volatile facts; unsupported fallback estimators are not equivalent substitutes.

skill_name.description: Skill or prompt name that produced the brief.

user_question_summary.description: Concise summary of the user's question, preserving unresolved empirical choices.

research_domain.description: Applied domain, such as environmental economics, climate economics, energy economics, finance economics, or a user-specific domain.

research_brief.description: Structured intake brief for units, timing, variables, estimands, design flags, risks, and next files. It is a scoping artifact, not paper-ready evidence.

user_question_rewrite.description: Cautious rewrite of the research question without unsupported causal, legal, audit-grade, or backend-certified language.

likely_design.description: Early design flags only; these do not certify identification or replace diagnostics.

binary_policy.description: Whether the treatment appears to be a binary policy or event indicator.

identification_risk_array.description: Threats such as selection, anticipation, spillovers, simultaneity, attrition, weak instruments, or measurement error.

diagnostic_or_design_response.description: Tentative mitigation or check; it must not be described as sufficient proof by itself.

method_array.description: Candidate methods to consider later; inclusion is not endorsement and does not bypass claim_gate.json.

blocked_method_array.description: Methods that are not recommended, premature, or fragile in the present setting.

diagnostic_array.description: Diagnostics that inform design credibility; diagnostic_success alone is not paper-ready evidence.

source_need_array.description: Facts requiring official/latest sources, especially policy, regulation, standard, API, and data-release facts.

action_array.description: Repository or coding actions. parser-only, interface-only, or missing_dependency states must not be labeled as backend passes.

claim_language.description: Allowed and avoided wording; strong causal, structural, legal, audit-grade, or backend-certified claims require support and claim_gate.json.

scholarly_depth.description: Depth of research discussion; more depth does not waive diagnostics, official/latest sourcing, or claim_gate.json.
